Erawan Cave
data.introduction: The cave mouth is in a low slope until the cavern opening. Passing the low-slope cave mouth is a large chamber where sunlight cannot reach some areas. The chamber is fragmented into various small chambers where beautiful stalactites and stalagmites are on display.

data.name: Erawan Cave
data.introduction: Situated close to Kaeng Luang, Tham Erawan cave is renowned as the most beautiful cave in Phrae province with the depth of 200 metres. Inside are chambers filled with stalatites and stalagmites which closely resembling Erawan elephant and the pregnant woman in according to the folklore of this cave.

data.name: Wat Ming Mueang
data.introduction: It is located on Suriyapong Road and enshrines the City Pillar of Nan province, which is kept in the tetrahedron pavilion in front of the Ubosot. The City Pillar is 3 metres high. The base is decorated with wood carved with lacquer patterns and gilded with gold, and the top of the Pillar was carved into the image of Brahma. It has the name Metta, Karuna, Mutita, and Ubekkha.

Whether you are traveling to Bangkok for business or shopping, or visiting Bangkok's landmarks, the Boulevard Hotel Bangkok is the perfect place to travel to anywhere in Bangkok. Nearly reaching Within walking distance or just by train or subway. If you want to escape the chaos of city life, you can relax in the Boulevard Hotel Bangkok, enjoy delicious food at our 4 different restaurants or lie down by the pool. Or maybe just relax and recharge in our well-furnished room.

It is also convenient to take the expressway to the airport. And with excellent rooms, delicious restaurants, business center and fitness room, this 5-star hotel is an excellent choice for those who are visiting Bangkok. In addition, Sivara, on-site spa is a haven for the city, offering relaxing massages and treatments at the Amari Watergate Hotel. Located in Pratunam, it is ideal for a visit to the city and to experience the wonders of Bangkok. Moreover, the expressway to the airport can be reached in a few minutes.

data.name: Dhevan Dara Beach Villa Kuiburi
data.introduction: Located a 1-minute walk from the beach/waterfront, Dhevan Dara Beach Villa Kuiburi offers modern accommodation with free Wi-Fi and free shuttle service to Hua Hin Town and shopping areas. There is a swimming pool and fitness center. The hotel is just a 3-minute walk from the temple. It is a 20-minute drive from Praya Nakorn Cave. It is 2 hours 30 minutes by car from Suvarnabhumi International Airport. Studio features a flat-screen TV, minibar and safety deposit box. An en suite bathroom with a hairdryer. Conference rooms and library services are available, as well as a tour desk and bicycle rental. Guests can enjoy Thai and international dishes at the beachfront restaurant. Drinks are available at the beach bar and swimming Pool lounge

data.name: Ko Libong
data.introduction: Ko Libong, Trangs largest island is located in Tambon Libong and is part of the Libong Archipelago Wildlife Reserve whose headquarters are located here. With an area of 40,000 square kilometres, Ko Libong, which is a haven for holidaymakers, has many fishing villages where most of the residents are Muslims.

data.name: Hat Yao

Wat Na Phra That
data.introduction: This temple, first called Wat Hua Muang by the villagers, is located 1 km. west of Wat Phra Non Chaksi. It houses an ancient brick pagoda which was built during the ancient Khmers glory. The pagoda was later renovated during the early Ayutthaya period. This area is assumed to have been the ancient site of Sing Buri.

data.name: Krung Ching Sea Of Fog
data.introduction: Located at Ban Bang Nop, Tambon Krung Ching. The words “Krung Ching” come from the name Ching tree, a plant in the palm family that is most common in this region. As for “Khao Lek”, this comes from the fact that this area used to be a mine. Because it is adjacent to Khao Luang National Park and Khao Nan National Park, the sea of mist of Krung Ching is surrounded by unspoiled nature. This is suitable for tourists who love nature. This is also both a spot to watch the sea of mist and the sunrise in the morning. The community is open for tourism all-year round, and it is possible to drive up to park on the hill near the viewpoint. Then, walk a little further to reach the viewpoint.

data.name: Ko Khai
data.introduction: Located in Phru Nai subdistrict, to the north-east of Ko Yao Yai, Ko Khai is made up of three small private islands, namely Ko Khai Nai, Ko Khai Nui and Ko Khai Nok. These islands resemble a fried egg from an ariel view, and they occupy the ocean space between Ko Sirae (Phuket) and Ko Yao Yai (Phang-nga). The water is crystal clear with stunning coral reefs, making this island popular with Phuket tourists, as it’s a just a short 15-minute boat ride away.

sha.detail: Sirindhorn learning park is a tourist attraction and an outside-classroom education center where sci, tech and inno are integrated to explain Thai wisdom and traditional culture for new and enjoyable learning experiences.

data.name: Phra That Phanom
data.introduction: Phra That Phanom is located 50 kilometers from Nakhon Phanom town on Highway No. 212. It is the most important Buddhist site in the province. A 53-meters tall square-shaped pagoda, it was believed to have been built before the 12th century. The pagoda, which is believed to have originally been of Khmer design, was renovated through the years to its present Laotian design. The pagoda enshrines the holy relics of the Lord Buddha. On August 11, 1975 at 7 p.m., the entire pagoda collapsed after many days of rainstorm. The Thai public subsequently made considerable donations to rebuild the pagoda to its original splendor. People placed many valuables inside and placed a 110-kilogram solid gold spire on top of the pagoda.

data.name: Benchasiri Park
data.introduction: This compact park on Sukhumvit Road near Soi 24 was built to commemorate the 60th birthday of HM Queen Sirikit in 1992. It features a huge sculpture of commemorative coin illustrating an image of HM Queen Sirikit and 18 pieces of contemporary sculptures including childrens works.

data.name: Wat Mahathat Worawihan
data.introduction: Locally called Wat Na Phrathat or Wat Phra Si Rattana Mahathat, this temple stands proudly along Khao Ngu Road in Na Mueang Subdistrict. Believed to have been erected during the Dvaravati Period, around the 10th-11th Century, it coincided with the establishment of the old Ratchaburi city. Approximately in the 13th Century, a Khmer or Lop Buri sanctuary was constructed and placed over the temple. The new construction symbolized the city's central role in Khmer cosmology. Then, for fear that the sanctuary might collapse, a new Phra Prang was constructed at the beginning of the Ayutthaya era, around the 15th-16th Century.

data.name: Thai Wax Museum
data.introduction: Located at No. 43/2, Mu 1, Borommaratchachonnani Road (Pinklao-Nakhon Chai Si), at Km. 31, Khun Kaeo Subdistrict, the museum showcases exquisite and lifelike fibreglass wax figures created by Ms. Duangkaeo Phityakornsilp and a team of Thai artists. The museum’s mission is to preserve and promote Thai art and culture.

sha.name: THAI HUMAN IMAGERY MUSEUM
sha.detail: Be amazed by over 100 life-like sculptured figures at the Thai Human Imagery Museum.

data.name: Sirindhorn Peat Swamp Forest Nature Research and Study Centre
data.introduction: This last remaining peat swamp forest in Thailand spreads over 3 districts including Tak Bai, Su-ngai Kolok and Su-ngai Padi. Covering an area of 192 square kilometers, of which 80 square kilometers are dense forests, the swamp is rich in fauna and flora. Major waterways that pass through the area are Khlong Su-ngai Padi, Bang Nara River and Khlong To Daeng, from which the forest derives its name....

data.name: The King Taksin Shrine
data.introduction: It is located in Wat Lum Mahachai Chumphon, Taksin Road, Tambon Tha Pradu. There is a huge 300-year-old Sa Tue tree in front of the shrine.

sha.name: Mesook Farm (Agarwood Farmer Group)
sha.detail: Activities include: touring around the Agarwood farm, learning about all steps of Agarwood products, agricultural tourism farm, feeding animals, kayaking in Mesok lake, and shopping high-quality Agarwood products.

data.name: Thepphitak Phunnaram Temple
data.introduction: Located at Khao Si Siat Aa, in Tambon Klang Dong, the temple houses a huge seated Buddha image that is 27 meters wide and 45 meters high. The image is enshrined on a mountaintop that can be reached via a 1,250 steps representing the number of monks that gathered spontaneously to hear the Lord Buddha's first sermon. (an occasion that is commemorated on Magha Puja Day)

data.name: Wat Ko Kaeo Thudongkhasathan  or Wat Rahan
Sanctuary of Truth Museum
data.introduction: This Thai style castle with four gable ends is built almost entirely of teak wood and is the only totally carved teak building in the whole of the country.

data.name: Chiang Khan Walking Street (Thanon Chai Khong)
data.introduction: Located in Loei province, Chiang Khan’s Walking Street is a famous tourist attraction that stretches for more than 1,200 metres. Both sides of the road are filled to the brim with culture and charm, providing a rare insight into Thailand’s past.

data.name: Phraya Surin Phakdi Si Narong Changwang (Pum) Monument
data.introduction: Phraya Surin Phakdi Si Narong Changwang (Pum) Monument is located on Surin-Prasat Road, Tambon Nai Mueang. It was built to commemorate the first city founder, who was a very important person in the history of Surin.

data.name: Wat Sa Prasan Suk (Wat Ban Na Mueang)
Wat Tham Seua
data.introduction: Wat Tham Suea is located at tambon Muang Chum, 4 kilometres beyond Wachiralongkorn Dam. The temple is built in a mixed, splendid Thai-Chinese style and enshrines a huge Buddha image.

data.name: Ko Lanta
data.introduction: Mu Ko Lanta National Park is located in Lanta District. Its important attractions are Ko Lan Ta Yai, Ko Lan Ta Noi, Ko Ha Archipelago, Ko Rok Nai, Ko Rok Nok and Ko Ngai.

data.name: Wat Tham Si Mongkhon (Wat Tham Din Phiang)
data.introduction: Hidden away in Ban Bang Dong Noi in Pha Tang subdistrict, this cave is known by the locals as ‘Tham Phaya Nak’ or the cave of the Naga serpent. The entrance to the cave is marked by a shrine where visitors can pay their respects to Pho Pu In Nakarachand and Mae Ya Kade Nakarach and ask the guardian spirits for permission to enter the cave.

data.name: Sala Kaeo Ku (Wat Khaek)
data.introduction: Also known as Wat Khaek, this temple in the Samakhi community, Wat That subdistrict, serves as a place of worship and features some bizarre, eye-catching mortar sculptures.

Phra Nakhon Khiri Historical Park
data.introduction: Phetchaburis well-known landmark, the locally known as Khao Wang (Palace Hill) is located up on a 92-meter high verdant hill, in the city of Phetchaburi. It was built under the royal command of King Rama IV and in 1860 became his summer palace. The whole compound is comprised of royal halls, palaces, temples and other buildings which were elegantly constructed in a well-balanced Thai, neoclassical Western and Chinese architectural styles.

data.name: The Royal Paradise Hotel & Spa
data.introduction: A world class ambiance Patong hotel that embodies the best of Thai hospitality, excellent value and environmentally friendly hotel, certified as ISO 9001 and Green Leaf Hotel since 2003 is centrally located at Patong Beach within 5 minutes walking distance of shopping center (Jungceylon Department Store) Tourist area attraction (Bangla Road, Pub, Bars) and the beach.Phuket accommodation, The Royal Paradise Hotel & amp; Spa (Patong hotel) with it naturally endowed with breath-taking sea views and sweeping panoramas of undulating hill, ideal location, unmatched excellent service and timeless ambience of an exotic tropical resort, will provide you with a unique and authentic vision of this heavenly paradise.Enjoy spectacular breathtaking views of the Andaman Sea, its crescent bays and distant islands from our "Paradise wing" with provides luxurious in 248 guest rooms and " Royal wing" offers 102 luxurious rooms with tastefully unique Thai Design. Every room equipped with air-conditioned, most with a private balcony, full amenities, and with a Thai contemporary touch.Patong Hotel, The Royal Paradise Hotel & amp; Spa is ideal for honeymooners, families or business group. It's a world of relaxation with multiple recreational service offered, including diving, golfing, our leisure paradise encompasses a fitness center, sauna, swimming pool and "Paradise Thai spa" dedicated to relaxation as well as to the preservation of youth and beauty; or savour acclaimed cuisine of our chefs in one of our four theme restaurants.

data.name: Chulabhorn Dam (Nam Phrom Dam)
data.introduction: Chulabhorn Dam is at Tambon Thung Phra and dams the Phrom River. It is a rock-filled dam with an earth core. The dam is 700 metres long and 70 metres high. It is used to generate electricity and irrigation. Boat rentals are available to view the sights of the reservoir. To get to the dam, take the Chaiyaphum-Chum Phae road (Highway No. 201) to Nong Song Hong junction, then turn left onto Highway No. 2055, a total distance of 120 kilometres from Chaiyaphum.

data.name: Wat Pho Kao Ton (Wat Mai Daeng)
data.introduction: This temple is near the Monument of Bang Rachan Heroes. It was the base where the Bang Rachan villagers resisted the Burmese attacks. A replica of the ancient fortress, identical to the original, was constructed. In a chapel, there is the statue of Phra Achan Thammachot, who was both a monk and a leader of the Bang Rachan's heroes.

data.name: Wat Phra Thong
data.introduction: Situated on Thep Krasattri Road, Thep Krasattri sub-district, this temple is home to a large, gold-covered Buddha image known as Phra Phut. Only half of the image can be seen protruding above the ground and it is considered a sacred image for the local Buddhists. There is also a museum in the temple grounds, displaying many artifacts from the early days of Phuket including some protective clothing used by miners during the boom and some foot bindings worn by early female Chinese settlers.

data.name: Ko Kham
data.introduction: A tiny island, Ko Kham can be easily reached by motorboat or kayak from neighbouring Ko Mak. The distinguishing features are the black volcanic rocks in eye-catching shapes and formations dotted across the sand. Ko Kham is quiet and unspoiled with crystal clear waters, fine sand and coral reefs on the eastern side which are perfect for snorkelling and diving.

sha.name: Wat Uphai Phatikaram
sha.detail: The importance of this temple is that it is the place where Goddess Sam Pang is enshrined. In Thailand there are only 3 statues, one enshrined at Wat Kalayanamit (Thonburi side), Bangkok. data.name: Wat Lokkayasutha
data.introduction: This monastery is over a kilometer behind Wat Suanluangsopsawan adjacent to Wat Worachettharam. Accessible by the road inside the compound of the Distillery Plant, or through the road behind the Phlapphla Trimuk (three-gabled roof pavilion), the monastery is in the area of the Ancient Palace. Proceed past Wat Woraphot and Wat Worachettharam until reaching the open-air site of the large reclining Buddha, which is made of brick and covered with plaster, is approximately 42 meters long. The ruins of large hexagonal pillars near the image are believed to be the ruins of the Ubosot.

data.name: Wat Maha That (Yasothorn)
data.introduction: Wat Maha That is an important temple of the province in the town. The building of importance here is Phra That Yasothon or Phra That Anon, an ancient square pagoda with the top similar to that of Phra That Phanom. The architecture is in the Laotian style which was popular in the late Ayutthaya to early Rattanakosin periods. The pagoda houses the relics of Phra Anon. A state minister from Si Sattana Khanahut (Vientiane) who took migrants to settle here about 200 years ago constructed it around 1778.
